macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Beat Expectations but Spark Sell-Off in Tech Stocks
items.summary: Nvidia reported stellar quarterly profit growth exceeding estimates and provided upbeat revenue forecast, yet its stock experienced the worst day since last spring, dragging Nasdaq down 273 points to 22,878. Semiconductors slumped amid profit-taking after historic gains.

items.title: US Markets Mixed: Dow Steady at 49,499, S&P 500 Down to 6,909, Nasdaq Leads Declines
items.summary: The Dow edged up slightly while S&P 500 fell 37 points and Nasdaq dropped sharply amid tech weakness; Canadian TSX surged nearly 200 points to 34,502 led by technology sector strength.

items.title: Canadian Banks Report Profit Gains on Trading Revenue
items.summary: CIBC, Royal Bank of Canada, and TD Bank posted year-over-year profit increases driven by trading and market-related gains; shares reacted mixed with CIBC up 2.89%, RBC down 2.14%, TD up 1.64%.

items.title: Profit-Taking Hits Markets Despite Dip-Buying Support
items.summary: North American equities saw risk-off profit-taking after Nvidia's record earnings, with tech and semis hit hard, but dip-buyers emerged at 200-hour moving averages; defensives gained as HALO rotation continues ahead of month-end.

items.title: PCE Data and Tech Stocks in Focus Amid Volatile Session
items.summary: Latest PCE economic indicators and tech stock movements highlighted in market update, contributing to mixed performance across major indices on February 26.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Remains Range-Bound Amid Tech Stock Drag
items.summary: Bitcoin holds steady above $67,000 despite U.S. equities pressured by tech stocks, while Ethereum consolidates around $2,000 and XRP steadies at $1.40 showing cautious bullish bias.

items.title: CFG Surges 50% on Upbit Listing
items.summary: Centrifuge (CFG) jumped 50% after Upbit announced listing of CFG trading pairs; GWEI rose 25% following Bithumb KRW market launch.

items.title: GROK Meme Token Explodes 520% After Elon Musk Post
items.summary: Grok-inspired meme token gork surged over 520% intraday following Elon Musk's post 'Gork returns today'.

items.title: Uniswap Governance Vote to Extend Fee Switch Across L2s
items.summary: Uniswap launched voting to extend 'fee switch' to 8 Layer 2 networks, potentially boosting annual revenue by $27 million if approved.

items.title: OCC Proposes Tighter Stablecoin Incentive Rules
items.summary: U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) proposed rules tightening incentives and yields for stablecoins, implementing the GENIUS Act.

items.title: Indiana Bitcoin Rights Bill Passes Legislature
items.summary: Indiana's Bitcoin Rights Bill passed both chambers and awaits governor's signature; Netherlands plans to amend unrealized crypto gains tax bill.

items.title: TON Wallet Launches BTC/ETH/USDT Yield Vaults
items.summary: Telegram-integrated TON Wallet introduced yield vaults for BTC, ETH, and USDT, with USDT strategy offering up to 18% blended APY.

items.title: MetaMask Card Fully Launches in U.S.
items.summary: MetaMask Card launched in the U.S. with a metal card design and on-chain cashback features.

items.title: Stable, Decred, and Pippin Lead Altcoin Gains
items.summary: Altcoins Stable (STABLE), Decred (DCR), and Pippin (PIPPIN) rallied amid broader market caution, with Stable and Pippin nearing record highs.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Researchers Introduce Agent World Pipeline Generating 1,000 Synthetic Environments for Faster AI Agent Training
items.summary: A new system called Agent World creates 1,000 diverse synthetic everyday scenarios with database-backed states, enabling AI agents to train with strong out-of-distribution generalization on benchmarks, outperforming benchmark-specific training.

items.title: CODE-SHARP Framework Enables AI to Autonomously Discover and Evolve Unlimited Skills Without Human Rewards
items.summary: Researchers from Imperial College London and Sony present Continuous Open-ended Discovery and Evolution of Skills as Hierarchical Reward Programs (CODE-SHARP), using foundation models to build hierarchical skills that solve complex tasks 134% better than experts.

items.title: CEOs Rank AI as Top Business Risk Surpassing Geopolitics, Cyber Threats, and Economic Instability
items.summary: Conference Board survey reveals CEOs for the first time view AI as the biggest industry risk, with 60% of Fortune 500 leaders agreeing; CEO confidence hits highest since Q1 2025 amid rising capital spending plans.

items.title: Quantum Computing and AI Convergence Highlighted with Hybrid Algorithms Offering Near-Term Advantages
items.summary: Analysis explores quantum algorithms like HHL, QAOA, and Grover's providing speedups for ML tasks; hybrid quantum-classical methods show promise on NISQ devices, with fault-tolerant QC expected in 5-10 years to boost AI training.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: New York Sues Valve Over Loot Boxes Alleged as Illegal Gambling
items.summary: New York Attorney General Letitia James filed a lawsuit against Valve on Wednesday, accusing its loot boxes in games like Counter-Strike of being 'quintessential gambling' that targets children and violates state laws. The suit seeks restitution for players and fines triple Valve's alleged illegal gains.

items.title: GameSquare Expands Partnership with Capcom for Resident Evil Requiem Global Launch
items.summary: GameSquare announced an expanded partnership with Capcom on February 26 to support the global launch of Resident Evil Requiem, enhancing marketing and esports efforts for the upcoming title.

items.title: Pokémon Day 2026 Kicks Off with Livestreams, Auctions, and New Merchandise
items.summary: Pokémon's 30th anniversary on February 27 features a major Pokémon Presents livestream promising new games and updates, eBay's 30/30 Collection Auction of rare items, and special TCG drops at retailers like Walmart.

items.title: Digitain Secures Isle of Man iGaming Software Supplier License
items.summary: Digitain obtained an Isle of Man license in January 2026, highlighting the jurisdiction's growing appeal for iGaming providers seeking regulatory stability amid evolving global regulations.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Global Smartphone Market Faces Biggest Decline in 2026 Due to Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: IDC forecasts a 12.9% drop in smartphone shipments to 1.12 billion units in 2026, the largest ever, driven by surging memory chip prices from AI infrastructure demand; low-end Android makers will suffer most while Apple and Samsung gain share.[1][3]

items.title: Industrial Manufacturers to Double Automation of Key Processes by 2030
items.summary: PwC survey shows industrial manufacturers expecting to highly automate key processes rising from 18% to 50% by 2030, with production/operations and product design leading at 76% and 72% heavy use, focusing on growth and productivity.[2]

items.title: Konica Minolta Announces New Organizational Structure and Leadership Changes
items.summary: Effective April 1, 2026, Konica Minolta establishes Corporate Digital Transformation and Accounting/Finance Headquarters, reorganizes Imaging Solution Business, and appoints new executive vice presidents including Toshiya Eguchi and Shinichiro Oka.[4]
